### Comments (latest)
++++14614867713 dlwu
reran experiment and collected new data. New data is attached. We are still seeing same issue with imh0_subsktpwr going to 0W after running for extensive period of time.

++++14614868337 shreyasu
Hi David,  I have created a new debug patch that allows for in-depth visibility in the area that I'm suspecting the issue to be. Could you please load it and do the following capture: 1. Load the IFWI here: \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\debug\DMR\User\shreyasu\subsocket_energy_extra_logging\OKSDCRB1_86B_2025.47.4.01_0029.D60_8000097E_0.662.0_1P0_NonIPClean_Trace_DebugSigned_VIS_C05804A3_202512041651.bin 2. Load the pcudata collateral using the following command: import users.primecode.primecode_utils as prime prime.load_dbg_patch_collaterals(r” \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\debug\DMR\User\shreyasu\subsocket_energy_extra_logging”) 3. Add the following variables to your below tracing and capture it in excel for a while  (float) imh1.pcudata.shreyas_time_in_seconds; imh1.pcudata.shreyas_last_tsc_snapshot; (float) imh1.pcudata.shreyas_consumed_power; (float) imh1.pcudata.shreyas_delta_time_ms; (float) imh1.pcudata.shreyas_energy_delta; (float) imh1.pcudata.shreyas_agg_sub_socket_energy_delta; imh1.pcudata.shreyas_overflow_occurred; Important Note: Please use a similar conversion method that your script uses for power_consumed to convert the "float" values marked above. This is really important. Let me know if you want to chat about this more.  Thanks!

++++14614870667 dlwu
Data requested by Shreyas below has been collected on 1263. Data is attached.

++++14614872069 shreyasu
Thanks for the data. Here's a second patch with some changes subsocket_energy_extra_logging_v2: 1. Load the IFWI here: \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\debug\DMR\User\shreyasu\subsocket_energy_extra_logging_v2\OKSDCRB1_86B_2025.47.4.01_0029.D60_8000097E_0.662.0_1P0_NonIPClean_Trace_DebugSigned_VIS_C05804B9_202512051945.bin 2. Load the pcudata collateral using the following command: import users.primecode.primecode_utils as prime prime.load_dbg_patch_collaterals(r” \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\debug\DMR\User\shreyasu\subsocket_energy_extra_logging_v2”) 3. Add the following variables to your below tracing and capture it in excel for a while  (float) imh1.pcudata.shreyas_time_in_seconds; imh1.pcudata.shreyas_last_tsc_snapshot; (float) imh1.pcudata.shreyas_consumed_power; (float) imh1.pcudata.shreyas_delta_time_ms; (float) imh1.pcudata.shreyas_energy_delta; (float) imh1.pcudata.shreyas_agg_sub_socket_energy_delta; imh1.pcudata.shreyas_overflow_occurred; Important Note: Please use a similar conversion method that your script uses for power_consumed to convert the "float" values marked above. This is really important. Let me know if you want to chat about this more. 

++++14614874366 dlwu
The results for the second patch appear to resolve issue of imh0_subsktpwr going to zero over extended period of time. This is results after running for over 12 hours. raw data in Excel is atta
